LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS & INSTALLATIONS, INC.'S (ESI) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT OWE PLAINTIFF A DUTY OF CARE AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT START THE BAGGAGE CONVEYOR, WAS NOT ON THE FLOOR WHEN THE INJURY OCCURRED, DEFENDANT WAS MERELY WORKING ON DRAWINGS WHEN IT RETURNED TO THE WORKSITE ON JANUARY 10, 2011, DEFENDANT DID NOT SUPERVISE OR CONTROL PLAINTIFF'S WORK, AND PLAINTIFF NEVER REPORTED TO DEFENDANT. (UMF 16-22). IN OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. PLAINTIFF ARGUES THAT DEFENDANT AND BASS WERE JOINT VENTURERS ON THE PROJECT AND DEFENDANT IS VICARIOUSLY LIABLE FOR THE NEGLIGENCE OF MR. ZUNIGA, BASS' EMPLOYEE. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT RAISE A JOINT VENTURE THEORY OF LIABILITY, SEE SIMMONS V. WARE (2013) 213 CAL.APP.4TH 1035, 1049, AND PLAINTIFF CANNOT AVOID SUMMARY JUDGMENT BY RELYING ON UNPLEADED CLAIMS, SEE HUTTON V. FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE CO. (2013) 213 CAL.APP.4TH 486, 493. THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S PRIMARY ASSUMPTION OF THE RISK ARGUMENT. DEFENDANT'S AUTHORITIES ARE FACTUALLY DISTINGUISHABLE AND NO CASE HOLDS THAT A WORKER ON A CONSTRUCTION SITE ASSUMES ALL THE RISKS PRESENT AT THAT SITE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Lori B. Feldman, Esq. of the Law Offices of Lori Feldman, Tel. 415.492.2120, for defendant and moving party;
Joshua S. Markowitz, Esq. of Carcione Cattermole Dolinski Stucky Markowitz & Carcione, Tel. 650.367.6811 for plaintiff and opposing party;
Asim K. Desai, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel. 310.831.1007, for defendant Brock Solutions, via Court Call.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS & INSTALLATIONS, INC.'S (ESI) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court grants summary judgment. The undisputed evidence shows that defendant did not owe plaintiff a duty of care at the time of the accident. The evidence shows that defendant did not start the baggage conveyor, was not on the floor when the injury occurred, defendant was merely working on drawings when it returned to the worksite on January 10, 2011, defendant did not supervise or control plaintiff's work, and plaintiff never reported to defendant. (UMF 16-22). In opposition, plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff argues that defendant and Bass were joint venturers on the project and defendant is vicariously liable for the negligence of Mr. Zuniga, Bass' employee. The complaint does not raise a joint venture theory of liability, see Simmons v. Ware (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 1035, 1049, and plaintiff cannot avoid summary judgment by relying on unpleaded claims, see Hutton v. Fidelity National Title Co. (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 486, 493. The Court rejects defendant's primary assumption of the risk argument. Defendant's authorities are factually distinguishable and no case holds that a worker on a construction site assumes all the risks present at that site. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
